prompt: A surreal, photorealistic impossible architectural scene depicting Simpson's Paradox. A grand monumental staircase structure sweeps horizontally from left to right across the frame, clearly descending from an elevated position on the far left down into a deep dark abyss on the far right — establishing a strict left-to-right downward aggregate trajectory like an x-y plot. However, the staircase is constructed from three distinct, precisely separated marble sections with geometrically sharp, clean-cut junctions between them: a blue marble section on the left, a green marble section in the center, and a red marble section on the right. Each individual colored section clearly and visibly ascends upward within its own segment — every flight of stairs within each colored section goes UP. Yet the overall macro-structure unmistakably descends from left to right into darkness. Small human figures walk upward on each colored section. The figures on the blue section carry small lightweight bundles. The figures on the green section carry medium-sized loads. The figures on the red section carry large heavy sacks — subtly hinting at differing sample sizes. The central void beneath the structure plunges into extremely deep atmospheric blackness with thick volumetric shadow and fog, creating a sense of vertigo and inescapable gravitational pull. Dramatic chiaroscuro lighting from the upper left. Hyper-detailed stone textures on each colored marble section. No text, no numbers, no labels, no charts, no equations, no diagrams, no axes. Purely architectural and physical. Cinematic, haunting, museum-quality photograph.
visual_logic: Three color-coded marble staircases each visibly ascend, yet sweep left-to-right into deepening darkness — the aggregate structure descends while every subgroup climbs, embodying trend reversal upon aggregation.
